steps.specialNote: بَيْت اللّٰهِ (the House of Allah) — this is an Idhafah: بَيْت is the first word (mudaf), اللّٰهِ is the second (mudaf ilayhi). The first word loses tanwin and the second takes kasra.

ayahCards.arabicText: وَإِذْ جَعَلْنَا الْبَيْتَ مَثَابَةً لِّلنَّاسِ وَأَمْنًا
ayahCards.translation: And when We made the House a place of return for the people and a place of security.

steps.specialNote: عَبْدُ اللّٰهِ = 'servant of Allah' — a classic Idhafah and the most honourable human title in Islam. The Prophet ﷺ is called عَبْدُهُ (His servant) in the Quran.

ayahCards.arabicText: سُبْحَانَ الَّذِي أَسْرَىٰ بِعَبْدِهِ لَيْلًا مِّنَ الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ
ayahCards.translation: Exalted is He who took His servant by night from Al-Masjid Al-Haram.

steps.specialNote: أَهْلُ الْكِتَابِ (People of the Book) is a famous Idhafah in the Quran referring to Jews and Christians. أَهْل is often the first word of Idhafah constructions.

ayahCards.arabicText: مَا يَوَدُّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ أَهْلِ الْكِتَابِ
ayahCards.translation: Neither those who disbelieve from the People of the Book…

steps.question: In عَبْدُ اللّٰهِ, why does اللّٰهِ end in ـِ (kasra)?
choices: It is the second word of an Idhafah — always majroor
It is the subject of the sentence
Allah's name always ends in kasra
steps.explanation: In Idhafah (possessive construction), the second word (mudaf ilayhi) is always Majroor — shown by kasra ـِ. The first word (mudaf) loses tanwin and cannot take الـ.
